condition.name: Gout
condition.note: Uric acid checked quarterly. Flares treated at onset per plan.

condition.name: Prediabetes
condition.note: Managed by diet and exercise, no medication. A1c 5.7 percent at last check.

condition.name: Mild osteoarthritis, both knees
condition.note: Activity modification and occasional acetaminophen. No running.

medication.name: Allopurinol
medication.instruction: Do not skip. Take with a full glass of water.

medication.name: Colchicine
medication.when: At the first sign of a gout flare
medication.instruction: Start within 24 hours of onset. Call the office if no improvement in 48 hours.

medication.name: Naproxen
medication.when: With colchicine during a flare, per flare plan

medication.name: Acetaminophen
medication.instruction: No more than 6 tablets in 24 hours.

standingRules.rule: Water all day. Dehydration is the most reliable flare trigger on record.
No beer, no spirits. An occasional glass of red wine is the agreed exception.
No shellfish, no organ meats. Limit red meat to small portions.

flare.outcome: Treated per plan, resolved in five days

appointment.note: Lab draw the week before, uric acid check.
